column1: # When dispatchFnDef runs an fn body it pushes the matched args (in
# sig order) onto the registry's argsStack. The 0-arg `args` word
# returns that frame as a List, so a body can read positional args
# by index without naming each param.
# The frame pops automatically when the body returns (via the
# try/finally in dispatchFnDef), and `args` outside any fn call
# Single-arg fn — body uses args lengthq / first.
def f1 ( fn [ a:Integer ] [ Integer ] [ args lengthq ] ) 5 f1
def f2 ( fn [ a:Integer ] [ Integer ] [ args firstq ] ) 7 f2
# Two-arg fn — args list reflects sig order.
def f3 ( fn [ a:Integer b:Integer ] [ Integer ] [ args lengthq ] ) 3 f3 4
def f4 ( fn [ a:Integer b:Integer ] [ Integer ] [ args firstq ] ) 3 f4 4
column3: sig[0] = forward arg (4) — same as a

column1: def f5 ( fn [ a:Integer b:Integer c:Integer ] [ Integer ] [ args lengthq ] ) 1 2 3 f5
column3: all-stack call form; args still has 3 entries
